title: Braised Lamb Tagine
description: A traditional North African dish slow-cooked in a rich and aromatic stew. This lamb tagine is a hearty and flavorful meal perfect for special occasions.

ingredients.name: ras el hanout spice blend
ingredients.notes: available at Middle Eastern markets or online

instructions: Heat the oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Brown the lamb in batches, about 5 minutes per side. Transfer the lamb to a plate and set aside.
Add the onion to the pot and cook until it's softened and lightly browned, about 8 minutes.
Add the garlic and cook for 1 minute, until fragrant.
Add the diced tomatoes, chicken broth, honey, and spice blend. Stir to combine.
Return the lamb to the pot and bring the mixture to a boil.
Reduce the heat to low and simmer, covered, for 2 1/2 hours, or until the lamb is tender and the sauce has thickened.
Season the stew with salt to taste.
Serve the tagine hot, garnished with chopped parsley.
